mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL levels signal accelerating inflation expectations, prompting market participants to price in tighter future monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS exceeding 300,000 signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating immediate positive momentum in S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >55% of triggered months, or signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad-based consumer and housing demand expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle signals weakening industrial output that outweighs rate hike benefits, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize in >50% of tightening-phase declines, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in FEDFUNDS below 0.25% triggers liquidity-driven buying as investors anticipate aggressive central bank support, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances when the threshold is crossed, or signal decays to zero predictive power after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS indicates labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher GDPC1 levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of high-GDP quarters, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating industrial production that improves near-term earnings outlooks, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <45% of triggered months, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.
